ogTitle: Mount Coot-tha vs Mount Cotton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Coot-tha and Mount Cotton.
heading: Mount Coot-tha vs Mount Cotton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Coot-tha, Queensland and Mount Cotton, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Coot-tha has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Mount Cotton (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Coot-tha or Mount Cotton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Coot-tha has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Mount Cotton scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Coot-tha scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Cotton scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Coot-tha or Mount Cotton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Coot-tha has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
